title:
Patient Report
Redesign
subtitle: From a blank page into a structured, shareable communication tool.

slides.title:
Patient Report
Redesign
slides.description: After every scan, doctors had to generate a patient report from a blank page. We rebuilt it into a template-driven system that produces professional reports in minutes.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: A blank page after every appointment
slides.content: No structure, no templates, no annotation. A third of the screen was always locked to static info doctors couldn't act on while editing.
issues: Three-panel layout — sidebar wasted 25% of screen space
Share and download buttons buried in the sidebar
No templates — every report started blank
Raw scan images with no annotation or tooth context
PDF output not designed for patients to read
slides.highlight: The interface was designed around the tool — not the doctor using it.

image.caption: Old — three-panel split, static sidebar, blank content area

slides.label: User Research
slides.title: What doctors told us
slides.content: Interviews across multiple practices. Same problems everywhere — accepted as workarounds, not reported as issues.

quotes.text: I just skip the report most of the time. It takes too long.
quotes.author: Dr. A, General Practice

quotes.text: Patients ask me to explain things the report doesn't show.
quotes.author: Dr. B, Restorative

quotes.text: I have no way to circle what I'm talking about on the image.
quotes.author: Dr. C, Orthodontics

quotes.text: Every report looks different — there's no standard starting point.
quotes.author: Dr. D, Implantology

slides.label: Design Goals
slides.title: What we set out to fix.

goals.title: Give the canvas back
goals.description: Move metadata and actions to the header — full screen for editing.

goals.title: Give doctors a starting point
goals.description: Procedure templates so reports start structured, not blank.

goals.title: Make findings visual
goals.description: Annotation and tooth tagging — show, don't describe.

kpis: Time to create a complete report

slides.label: UI Layout
slides.title: From three fixed panels to a full editing canvas

afterImage.caption: New — compact header, full canvas for editing

slides.beforeDescription: A permanent right sidebar locked doctor info, preview, and share buttons into 25% of the screen.
slides.afterDescription: Everything moved to a compact top header — doctor, clinic, patient, and actions inline.
beforeBullets: Sidebar permanently occupied with static info
Share and download buried in the sidebar
Content squeezed between two fixed panels
afterBullets: Doctor, clinic, and patient editable from the header
Share and Export PDF always one click away
Full screen dedicated to report content
slides.highlight: The header replaced an entire panel — and made every action faster to reach.

slides.label: Templates & Sections
slides.title: A structured starting point — built to flex
slides.content: Procedure templates pre-seed the report with the right sections. A modular block system lets doctors add, reorder, or remove anything to fit the case.
issues: Five templates — General Scan, Implant, Crown Prep, Follow-up, Custom
Each pre-seeded with the sections that procedure needs
Modular blocks — Image, Before/After, Clinical Details, Cost, Notes
Drag to reorder, one-tap duplicate or delete
slides.highlight: Templates give doctors a running start — sections let them stay in control.

image.caption: Template picker and modular section builder

slides.label: Annotation & Tooth Chart
slides.title: From plain images to clinically annotated findings
slides.content: Doctors annotate directly on scan images and tag findings to specific teeth via an interactive FDI chart.
issues: Pen, text, and color tools — annotate full-size before saving
FDI chart — click to tag teeth, shown as numbered pills
Patients see exactly which teeth are affected
slides.highlight: Two tools that answered the same question: where exactly is the problem?

image.caption: Annotation canvas and FDI tooth chart

slides.label: Share & Export
slides.title: From three basic buttons to a full sharing system

beforeImage.caption: Old — Preview, Download PDF, Share via QR

afterImage.caption: New — Share Report modal with access control and multi-channel delivery

slides.beforeDescription: Three sidebar buttons — Preview, Download PDF, Share via QR. No access control, no recipient management.
slides.afterDescription: A full Share Report modal — email invites, access permissions, PIN protection, multi-channel delivery.
beforeBullets: Three isolated actions with no shared logic
QR code was the only real sharing option
No way to invite specific recipients
afterBullets: Invite by email with ownership and access levels
PIN protection toggle for sensitive reports
Multi-channel delivery — WhatsApp, WeChat, QR, copy link
Privacy warning built into the flow
slides.highlight: Sharing stopped being an afterthought — it became a controlled, secure handoff.

slides.label: Doctor Feedback
slides.quote: The templates actually match what I do, and everything I need is right there in front of me.
slides.author: Dr. N. Haddad

outcomes.title: Report creation
outcomes.description: Reports that took 15+ minutes now start in seconds.

outcomes.title: Clinical communication
outcomes.description: Annotation and tooth tagging let doctors show, not describe.

outcomes.title: Completion rate
outcomes.description: With structure upfront, doctors stopped skipping the report.

outcomes.title: Reach per report
outcomes.description: Multi-channel sharing reached patients on their preferred channel.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Designing clinical tools that work at the speed of care.
